question: According to Table 5.1-2, what is the cold leg temperature at 100% power?

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance, focusing on optimizing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures to maximize electrical generation and maintain safety margins. Calculations using NRELAP5 software, supported by hand calculations, establish best-estimate, maximum, and minimum flow conditions while accounting for uncertainties and ensuring low pressure losses to support natural circulation in the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including the RPV upper shell, baffle plate, heater bundles, and associated components. It specifies the use of low alloy steel, various stainless steels, and nickel-based alloys, all in accordance with relevant BPVC Section II material specifications, to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes the main components of the NuScale reactor system, focusing on the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping, and the Pressurizer (PZR). The RPV serves as a primary barrier to fission product release and houses key reactor internals, while the RCS piping connects various subsystems and the PZR is integrated into the RPV to help control system pressure. Each component's functions and connections are briefly outlined, with references to more detailed sections.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems in the NuScale Power Module, detailing the integration of the reactor, steam generators, pressurizer, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV). It also outlines the configuration and boundaries of the steam generator system, emphasizing the integral design of the steam generators within the RPV and the separation between primary and secondary sides.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains water chemistry, and ensures uniform boron concentration to protect fuel integrity during all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module (NPM), which integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single reactor pressure vessel (RPV) surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component, supporting various reactor systems and providing a barrier to fission product release, with materials and fabrication standards selected to ensure long-term integrity and compliance with ASME codes.

edges.description: Section A discusses the establishment of steady state values for primary and secondary side parameters as a function of reactor power, which are the types of data summarized in Table 5.1-2, while Section B provides a general description of the reactor coolant system whose parameters (such as cold leg temperature) are tabulated in Table 5.1-2.
Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.4 for further description of the steam generators (SGs), establishing a structural connection between the two sections.
Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system design, while Section B gives detailed material specifications for the pressurizer, which is a component described in Section A.

Section A describes specific operational procedures and component functions, while Section B provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system design, with Section A specifying details for the general system described in Section B.

Section B explains that steady state values for parameters like cold leg temperature at various power levels are established and references Table 5.1-2, while Section A provides general design information about the reactor coolant system but does not specify parameter values.
Section A provides detailed descriptions of the reactor coolant system components, which are part of the general system overview described in Section B.

Section B provides references to other sections for material specifications of pressurizer components mentioned in Section A's general system description, connecting the detailed material information to the overall system design.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system, while Section B discusses the evaluation of system performance parameters (such as cold leg temperature) at various power levels, connecting the general system description to the specific operational data found in Table 5.1-2.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system, while Section B references specific components and materials of the pressurizer, which is included as part of the system described in Section A.

Section B explains that steady state values for parameters like cold leg temperature at various power levels are established and tabulated (e.g., in Table 5.1-2), which is the source for the answer to the analysis question, while Section A provides general context about the reactor vessel but does not contain the specific data.

Section A describes the reactor vessel as a component of the reactor coolant system (RCS), while Section B provides a summary of the RCS, explicitly stating that the RCS includes the reactor pressure vessel described in Section A.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor vessel and its role in the reactor coolant system, while Section B details the pressurizer components and materials, referencing the reactor vessel as part of its structure.

Section B describes the reactor vessel as a component of the reactor coolant system (RCS), which is generally described in Section A, and explicitly states that the RCS is described in Section 5.1 (Section A).
